description: Forces the ballista gun nearest to the calling admin player to reload immediately; admin-only
Generate a performance capture containing the next run of updateMissionValidStateWorkQueue
Deals the specified amount of damage to the battering ram head entity nearest to the calling admin player; admin-only
Clears all radio station data set by the given Steam64 ID from all deployed and held boomboxes on the server
Clears the saved audio recording from all cassette items currently on the server; returns count of cassettes cleared
Clears saved audio from all cassettes created by the given Steam64 ID; returns count of cassettes cleared
Fire all catapults
Reload all catapults. 0: empty, 1: stone boulder, 2: fire bomb, 3: propane explosive, 4: bee bomb, 5: bot player
Commands all active CH47 Chinook helicopter AI controllers to drop their cargo crate immediately
Hides cinematic entities by group (0= none, 1= lights, 2= BGs, 3= props, 4= misc)
Prints pool statistics for the camera renderer manager including active renderer count, pooled task count, and task creation/return counters
<name/id> <radius> | Use print_wallpaper_skins for a list | 0 -> default, -1 -> random
Clears all UGC content (images, patterns) from the entity with the given network ID and notifies the IUGCBrowserEntity component
Triggers a full refresh of the admin UI by requesting the player list, server info, convars, and UGC list all at once
Sends the firework pattern design data for the specified pattern firework entity to the requesting admin client
Server-side handler that serialises and sends the current player list to the requesting admin client for display in the admin UI
Server-side handler that collects all ServerAdmin+ShowInAdminUI convars and sends them to the admin client for editing via the admin UI
Server-side handler that serialises and sends current server info (name, players, FPS, etc.) to the requesting admin client
Server-side handler that retrieves a specific UGC data blob by CRC, entity ID, and type and sends it to the requesting admin client
Server-side handler that scans all entities for UGC content (images, patterns, vending names) and sends a serialised list to the admin client
Applies the given genes (e.g. "YYYGGG") to the clone/seed in your hands
Returns all entities that the provided player is authed to (TC's, locks, etc), supports --json
Authorises the specified player (or caller if none given) to all tool cupboards within the given radius around them
Authorises multiple specified players to all tool cupboards within the given radius around the calling admin
Finds all players within playerRadius of the caller, then authorises each of them to TCs within authRadius of themselves
ban <player> <reason> [optional duration]
banid <steamid> <username> <reason> [optional duration]
List of banned users (sourceds compat)
List of banned users - shows reasons and usernames
List of banned users
Get information about this build
Get information about all the cars in the world
<name/id> <radius> | Use print_wallpaper_skins for a list | 0 -> default, -1 -> random
clear_wallpaper_radius <radius>
Removes all items inside the container you're looking at
clearcontainer_radius <radius>: Removes all items inside a container within a radius
Clears UGC content from all entities that have the specified player (by name or Steam ID) in their editing history
Clears UGC content from all entities within the given radius of a world position; reports how many entities were cleared
Clears all UGC content from a single entity by network ID; reports success or failure
Clears the custom name UGC from all vending machines whose content string contains the given search text (case/symbol insensitive)
Requests a performance report from every connected client; supports legacy and JSON formats; used for monitoring client frame rates and memory usage
Removes authorisation for the specified player (or caller) from all tool cupboards within the given radius
Removes authorisation for multiple specified players from all tool cupboards within the given radius around the calling admin
Finds all players within playerRadius of the caller, then deauthorises each of them from TCs within authRadius of themselves
Returns all entities that the provided player has placed, supports --json
Runs an admin command (kill, lock, unlock, etc.) on a specific entity by network ID; blocks operation on players and point entities
Fills the container you are looking at with random items, can also specify a category (ammunition, weapon etc.)
Fills containers with random items within a radius, can also specify a category
Fills your inventory with random items, can also specify a category (ammunition, weapon etc.)
Returns a JSON object containing the UGC info (CRCs, type, player history) for the entity with the given network ID
Puts the specified player into the wounded/downed state immediately without killing them; useful for testing the crawl/revive mechanics
Kicks the specified player from the server with an optional reason; broadcasts the kick to chat and places them through the queue on reconnect
Kicks all currently connected players from the server with an optional reason; useful for forcing a restart or clearing the server
Deals lethal damage to every non-NPC player currently connected to the server; reports the number of players killed
Kills all bee swarms
Deals 1000 damage to the specified player (by name/SteamID/bot) killing them immediately; useful for testing death logic without console kill commands
List of banned users, by ID (sourceds compat)
Adds the specified Steam64 ID as a server moderator with optional name and reason; grants admin flag to the player if connected
Adds the specified Steam64 ID as a server owner (auth level 2) with optional name and reason; requires the caller to also be auth level 2
Get a list of players
Get a list of player's IDs
Print out currently connected clients etc
Lists all wallpaper skins
Recovers the specified player from the wounded state, standing them back up at minimum health
Removes moderator status from the specified Steam64 ID; removes admin flag from the player if currently connected
Removes owner status from the specified Steam64 ID; removes admin flag from the player if currently connected
Removes skip queue permission from a SteamID
Sends a message in chat
Get a list of information about the server
Silently kicks the specified player without broadcasting to chat; the kick is logged to RCON only
<skin>
skin_radius 'skin' 'radius'
Moves the specified Steam64 ID to the front of the connection queue so they connect immediately on next join
Adds skip queue permissions to a SteamID
Show user info for players on server.
Show user info for sleeping players on server in range of the player.
Print out stats of currently connected clients
Print out currently connected clients
Prints a table of all members in the team of the specified player showing Steam ID, username, online status, and whether they are team leader; supports --json
Removes the ban for the specified Steam64 ID from the server banlist, allowing the player to reconnect
<grade>
upgrade_radius 'grade' 'radius'
Show user info for players on server.
Show user info for players on server in range of the player.
Show user info for players on server in range of the supplied player (eg. Jim 50)
Add a player (or command user if no player is specified) to the AIs ignore list.
Prints the total count of registered AIInformationZone instances on the server
Prints the current count of active animal, scientist, pet, and new NPC2 brain instances on the server
Remove all players from the AIs ignore list.
Kills all animal NPCs and animal NPC2 entities currently on the server
Kills all scientist NPCs, tunnel dwellers, and non-animal NPC2 entities currently on the server
Print a lost of all the players in the AI ignore list.
Print a list of all scientists that are off the navmesh. Optionally kill them off by passing true as the first argument.
Remove a player (or command user if no player is specified) from the AIs ignore list.
Editor-only: finds the NPC entity with the given network ID on the server and selects its game object in the Unity editor hierarchy
The time it takes for a NPC to fully notice a player standing right in front of them, in seconds.
Editor-only: teleports the caller to a top-down view, fills NPC populations, then draws DDraw labels and lines showing each NPC category and closest neighbour
Prints statistics about AI sleeping zones: how many zones are sleepable, how many are sleeping, and the total count of sleeping entities
Wakes all currently sleeping AI information zones, forcing all sleeping NPCs within them to become active; reports zones and entity counts woken
Bans a player by Steam ID from the server via the app layer, adding them to the banlist and kicking them if connected
Removes a ban for the specified Steam ID from the server banlist, allowing the player to reconnect
Prints current server info including name, level, connected players, max players, and network address to the console
Retry initializing the Rust+ companion server if it previously failed
Sets the ducked/crouching model state on a specific bot by name or Steam ID; used to control bot posture in testing scenarios
Executes a console command on a specific bot player by name or Steam ID; hidden from admin UI as it is intended for bot scripting only
Executes a console command on every bot player currently on the server; hidden from admin UI
Executes a console command on all bot players within a given radius of the calling admin; hidden from admin UI
Forces an immediate Bradley APC respawn, bypassing the normal respawn delay; admin only
Prints info about a clan given its ID
Server-side handler that copies all entities within the specified bounding box (center + size) into a named paste file; called from copybox client command
Server-side handler that copies all entities belonging to the building the player is looking at into a named paste file; called from the copybuilding client command
Server-side handler that copies all entities within the specified radius around a position into a named paste file; called from the copyradius client command
Downloads a paste file stored on the server (legacy server-side storage) by name and sends its entity data to the requesting client for local storage
Server-side handler that destroys all entities within the current selection bounds that match the active paste filter options (NPCs, vehicles, deployables etc.)
Lists all paste files stored in the legacy server-side copypaste directory and prints their names to the console
Server-side handler that prints the names of all entities within the current selection bounds; used to preview what would be included in a copy operation
Undoes the most recent paste operation for the calling player by destroying all entities that were spawned in that paste; replies with 'History empty' if nothing to undo
Sets the always-on state for all IAlwaysOn entities on the server (e.g. lights, switches); only works when alwaysOnEnabled is true and caller is in creative mode
Enables or disables creative mode for a specific player by name or Steam ID; creative mode removes resource costs and unlocks building freely
Exports a named terrain map layer (splatmap, heightmap, biomemap, topologymap, alphamap, watermap) to a .raw file in the persistent data path
Sends a ddraw.text debug draw command to a specific player by Steam ID or name; allows admins to display debug text overlays on another player screen
Sets the visual variant index on the building block the calling player is looking at; useful for testing block randomisation visuals
Applies a debug reset time in seconds to all PuzzleReset objects in the scene, shortening their timers for rapid testing
Spawn lots of IO entities to lag the server
Break the current held object
Almost break the current held object
Break all the items in your inventory whose name match the passed string
Breaks the currently held shield
Removes all active modifiers (buffs/debuffs) from the calling player; useful for resetting modifier state during testing
If a player ends up stuck on a tutorial for any reason this will clear the island and reset the player (will also kill player)
Completes all incomplete objectives in the calling player active mission, triggering the mission completion flow
Immediately completes the calling player tutorial by triggering the island completion callback; bypasses normal progression
Prints a message to the server console using the specified ConsoleColor index; useful for testing coloured console output
Logs a configurable number of test messages of a given length; used to stress-test console/logging performance and measure output speed
Prints the world position of every industrial conveyor running in strict mode; helps locate conveyors that are blocking item flow
Delete entities with the given short prefab name
Delete entities by id. Supports multiple arguments
Adds a specified amount of hydration to the calling player at a configurable rate; useful for quickly testing thirst-related mechanics
Drops a specified number of the given item short name as world entities from just in front of the calling player; useful for item physics testing
Adds a specified amount of calories to the calling player at a configurable rate; useful for quickly testing hunger-related mechanics
Sends a client RPC to the target player enabling or disabling their movement controls; admin only; useful for testing freeze/lock mechanics
Spawns and mounts a player on every mount point in radius
Takes you in and out of your current network group, causing you to delete and then download all entities in your PVS again
Deducts the given number of hours from all spoilable food on the server
Applies the given number of hours to all food in the players inventory
Spoils all food on the server
Forces all chickens within a given radius of the calling player to immediately spawn an egg; useful for testing egg drop and collection logic
To disable revival if player is downed, pass in --no-recover as the first argument.
Deals a specified amount of bullet damage to the calling player; optionally targets a named bone to test per-bone hit reactions
Prints out the topologies at your position
Prints the network visibility layer (overworld, tunnel, underwater, etc.) at the calling player position; helps debug layer-based network group assignment
Logs all server entity network group IDs and prefab names to the console; useful for debugging network visibility and group assignment
Prints a table of all ObjectWorkQueue instances showing name, total items processed, current queue length, and cumulative execution time
Reset all puzzles. Optionally provide a number to only reset puzzles within a radius.
Prints detailed debug info about the PuzzleReset the calling player is currently inside, including timer state and dependency status

Refills the vital of a target player. eg. debug.refillsvital jim - leave blank to target yourself, can take multiple players at once. Will revive players if they are injured. To disable this, pass in --no-recover as the first argument.
Refills the vitals of all active players on the server. Will revive players if they are injured. To disable this, pass in --no-recover as the first argument.
Scans all static respawn areas and kills any whose centre is within 1 metre of another, eliminating duplicate spawn points
Generates and logs a render info report showing draw calls, batch counts, triangle counts, and shadow caster counts for the current frame
Repair all items in inventory
Resets all sleeping bag respawn cooldown timers for the calling player, allowing immediate re-use of all their bags
Respawns vine trees from their stumps within a given radius of the calling player; reports how many were respawned versus blocked by players
Deals enough bullet damage to bring the calling player or a named target to the specified health value
Sets the calling player or a named target player calorie level to the specified value directly
Sets the calling player or a named target player health to the specified value; useful for testing low-health or death scenarios
Overrides the maximum health of the calling player or a named target; pass 0 to reset to the default value
Sets the calling player or a named target player radiation level to the specified value directly
0 = can't throw, 1 = can throw & melee, 2 = only throwable
Sets the ore fill percentage on all unloadable train cars within 3 metres of the calling player; updates both inventory amounts and visual ore level
Sets the calling player or a named target player hydration level to the specified value directly
Spawns one of every deployable in a grid
Spawns a 100x10 grid of lit furnaces loaded with wood and metal ore near the calling player; used to stress-test the oven cooking system
Spawns a clone of the calling player at a configurable height with a parachute deployed and their belt and wear inventories copied
Stalls the main thread for the given duration in seconds (clamped 0-1); admin-only; used to test timeout handling and watchdog systems
Sends a configurable number of test custom vital entries to the calling player client for a given duration; used to verify custom vitals UI rendering
Logs a test error and exception for testing error display.
Triggers the tutorial island ending cinematic for the calling player; spawns a kayak at the designated mount point and mounts the player to it
Prints a table of all active tutorial islands showing index, network group ID, assigned player name, duration, and connection state
Sends a highlight RPC to every VineMountable on the server targeting the calling player; used for visually debugging vine placement
Prints a summary table of all VineSwingingTree and VineMountable entities on the server, including average destination count per mountable
Initiates the deep sea closing sequence (wipe), triggering the radiation end-phase and eventually destroying all deep sea entities; fails if already closed or busy
Creates the deep sea manager entity on the server, spawning all deep sea islands, ghost ships, and portals; kills any existing deep sea manager first if present
Teleports the calling player (or their mounted vehicle) into the deep sea dimension; requires deepsea.createdeepsea to have been run first
Teleports the calling player (or their mounted vehicle) back to the main island from the deep sea; fails if the player is not currently inside the deep sea
Initiates the deep sea opening sequence, activating portals and making the zone accessible to players; fails if already open or if an open/close is in progress
Prints a breakdown of all entities currently in the deep sea, including totals for players, NPCs, vehicles (by prefab), trees, and metal detector sources
Prints a breakdown of all loot containers, trees, and ore nodes currently present in the deep sea, grouped by prefab name with counts
Prints the current time remaining until the deep sea wipe triggers as a formatted string; useful for monitoring wipe countdown during testing
Sets the time in seconds until the deep sea re-opens after a wipe; useful for skipping the cooldown period during testing
Sets the time in seconds until the deep sea wipe triggers; pass 0 to trigger immediately; useful for testing the wipe sequence without waiting
Spawns a random floating city prefab at the calling player's XZ position in the deep sea; used to test city layout and encounter setup
Spawns a random ghost ship prefab at the calling player's XZ position in the deep sea; used to test ghost ship placement and encounter setup
Spawns a random deep sea island prefab at the calling player's XZ position in the deep sea; ignores Y so the island sits at sea level
Spawns a RHIB patrol boat group at the calling player's XZ position, oriented toward the deep sea centre; used to test boat AI and patrol behaviour
Prints the current deep sea status including open/busy state, time to next opening, time to wipe, radiation amount, portal direction, entrance transform, and all active portal positions
Tests whether the calling player's current position is a valid portal spawn location; draws a green sphere if valid or red sphere if invalid with the given radius and duration in seconds
Starts recording a server-side demo for the specified player by name or Steam ID to a timestamped file; the player must be connected
Stops the active server-side demo recording for the specified player and finalises the demo file
Toggles the debug info overlay for an entity by net ID, showing position, velocity, health, and network state in the world
Destroy all entities created by provided users (separate users by space)
Destroy all entities created by users in the provided text block (can use with copied results from ent auth)
Lists all networked entities whose prefab path contains the given filter string in a formatted table; admin-only on client
Lists all networked entities belonging to the given network group ID in a formatted table; admin-only on client
Lists the networked entity with the given network entity ID in a formatted table; admin-only on client
Lists all networked entities that have the given network entity ID as their parent in a formatted table; admin-only on client
Lists all networked entities within the given radius in metres of the calling player in a formatted table; admin-only on client
Lists all networked entities owned by the calling player (matched by network ID) in a formatted table; admin-only on client
Lists all networked entities whose status string contains the given filter text in a formatted table; admin-only on client
Applies a small positional nudge to an entity by net ID, useful for unsticking entities that are clipping into geometry
Sets the charge level of an electric battery entity by net ID to the given percentage (0-100)
Spawns a copy of the loot table from one container prefab into the world at the calling player position
Spawns a server entity by prefab name at a given world position and direction; returns the spawned entity net ID
Spawns a grid of server entities by prefab name centred at a position; useful for stress-testing entity counts
Spawns a dropped item entity server-side by item short name at a given world position
Advances the in-game time of day by the specified number of hours; useful for quickly cycling to day or night for testing
Prints a table of all registered server events with their current enabled state, minimum delay, maximum delay, and last trigger time
Enables or disables a specific server event by name; disabled events will not trigger automatically until re-enabled
Sets the maximum delay in seconds between automatic triggers of the named server event
Sets the minimum delay in seconds between automatic triggers of the named server event
Sets the active game mode by name; game modes can alter loot tables, rules, and player abilities (e.g. softcore, hardcore)
Allocates a byte array of the given size (default 1 MB) as a GC pressure test; useful for profiling memory allocation throughput
Triggers an immediate full managed garbage collection pass; useful after large allocations for memory profiling
Calls Resources.UnloadUnusedAssets() to unload assets no longer referenced by any object, freeing RAM and VRAM
Breaks all equipped clothing items currently worn by the calling player, reducing their condition to zero
Reduces the condition of all items in the calling player inventory whose short name matches the given string to zero, breaking them
Removes all spray paint entities from the server world; useful for cleaning up excessive player spray art
Removes all spray paint entities created by a specific player, identified by Steam ID or name
Removes all dropped item entities from the server, cleaning up every piece of loot on the ground
Removes all spray paint entities within a given radius of the specified world position (X Y Z)
Removes all spray paint entities within the given radius of the calling admin current position
Prints the count of enabled versus disabled Collider components currently in the scene
Clears prefab pools and releases pooled objects; delegates to pool.clear_prefabs; admin/developer only
Puts the calling player or a named target into the wounded/downed state, simulating the critical injury bleed-out state
Prints all live Unity Object instances sorted by total memory usage, showing type, instance count, and estimated total size in bytes
Prints all scenes registered in the build settings with their build index and asset path
Prints the current state of server-side stability check and surroundings update queues; reports nothing useful on client
Quits the application cleanly with no arguments; rejects calls with arguments to prevent accidental exit; in the editor exits play mode
Revives the calling player or a named target from the wounded state, restoring them to standing with a small amount of health
Runs a server performance diagnostic report covering entity counts, memory usage, and active invokes, outputting results to the server console
Schedules a server restart; optionally accepts a countdown in seconds and a broadcast message sent to all players before the restart occurs
Puts the calling player into the sleeping state, disconnecting their control and making them a sleeping entity on the server
Puts the player that the calling admin is looking at into the sleeping state; useful for testing sleeping player interactions
Enters spectator mode; optionally accepts a player name or Steam ID to spectate that specific player from a third-person camera
Enters spectator mode targeting the entity or player with the given network entity ID
Prints a table of active network group subscriptions for the calling player, showing realm and group ID; supports --json flag
Prints a summary of the current machine hardware and OS info including CPU, GPU, RAM, and platform
Prints the unique device identifier for the current machine as reported by Unity SystemInfo.deviceUniqueIdentifier
Teleports the calling admin to a player by name or partial name match; if two arguments are given, moves the first-named player to the second
Teleport to a random entity the player is authed on
Teleports the calling admin to their own most recent death location
Teleports the calling admin to the centre of the named map grid square (e.g. A1, B3)
Teleports the calling admin to the map marker they have placed on their in-game map
Teleports the named player to the calling admin current position
Teleports the calling admin to the target location of their currently active mission objective
Teleports the calling admin to an entity owned by a specified player, identified by Steam ID or name
Teleports the calling admin to the nearest entity matching the given prefab short name, with an optional radius filter
Teleports the calling admin and their player boat to the centre of the named map grid square (e.g. A1, B3)
Teleports the calling admin and their player boat to the map marker they have placed on their in-game map
Teleports the calling admin and their player boat to exact world coordinates specified as X Y Z arguments
Teleports all connected players to the calling admin current position
Teleports the calling admin to the point in the world that their line of sight is currently hitting
Teleports all connected active (non-sleeping) players to the calling admin current position
Teleports the calling admin or a named player to exact world coordinates specified as X Y Z arguments
Teleports all sleeping player entities to the calling admin current position
Teleports all members of the named player team to the calling admin current position
Teleports all members of the calling player team to the calling admin current position
Prints a list of all live Texture objects with their name and estimated runtime memory size
Toggles display of the team info overlay (health, location, vitals) for the spectated player while in spectator mode
Immediately update the manifest
Prints the current game version string to the console, including build number and branch
Loads and applies a Harmony patch assembly by file name, enabling server-side code patching without restarting
Unloads a previously loaded Harmony patch assembly by file name, reverting any code modifications it applied
Changes the current hierarchy context to the named child GameObject, similar to the Unix cd command; allows drilling into nested scene objects
Deletes the named GameObject from the scene hierarchy; use with caution as this permanently removes the object
Lists all GameObjects in the current hierarchy context, similar to the Unix ls command; used for navigating the scene hierarchy from the console
Add ownership to item
Clears the inventory of a target player. eg. inventory.clearInventory jim. Can take container names as arguments: --belt --wear --backpack
Reduce ownership to item to allow new ownership to be added
Copies the players inventory to the player in front of them
Prints the names of all Steam inventory item definitions currently loaded from the Steam backend; useful for verifying skin/item definition state
Deletes a named admin loadout file from the server; requires admin or developer permissions; loadout name is passed as the first argument
Deploys the given loadout to a target player. eg. inventory.deployLoadout testloadout jim
Deploys a loadout to players in a radius eg. inventory.deployLoadoutInRange testloadout 30
Equips the belt/hotbar item at the given slot index for the calling admin or a named target player; requires admin or developer permissions
Equips the belt/hotbar slot at the given index for the player the admin is currently looking at; requires admin or developer permissions
{item} {amount} {condition} {skin} {container} {slot}
Gives the specified item (by partial name) to every currently connected player on the server; broadcasts who issued the command in chat
{itemid} {amount}
Gives the calling player a blueprint item for the specified item (by partial name); broadcasts the gift in chat unless the recipient is a developer
{item}
{itemid} {amount}
{item} {player} {amount} {skin}
Prints all saved inventory loadouts
Reads the list of saved admin loadouts from disk and sends it to the requesting player's client as a JSON array for display in the loadout UI
Checks whether the calling player has the specified item ID with the given skin ID in their inventory; used to validate pipette/skin matching server-side
Reduce ownership to item to allow new ownership to be added
Forces a reload of all Steam inventory item definitions from the Steam backend; use if definitions appear stale or missing after a store update
Resets all blueprints for the specified player (by name/Steam ID) back to the default unlocked set, removing any learned recipes
Saves the current equipped loadout of the calling player. eg. inventory.saveLoadout loaduoutname
Sets the custom icon image ID on the item currently held by the calling player; the image ID refers to a server-side stored image used for custom item icons
Set worn items to have maximum armor slots supported
Unlocks every craftable blueprint for the specified player (by name/Steam ID), giving them access to all recipes immediately
Prints the Facepunch application manifest in a formatted summary showing app name, version, and platform details
Prints the raw contents of the Facepunch manifest file as an unformatted string
Takes a full Unity Memory Profiler snapshot capturing all managed, native, and graphics memory and saves it as a timestamped .snap file in profile/
Takes a Unity Memory Profiler snapshot capturing managed (C#) heap allocations and saves it as a timestamped .snap file in the profile/ folder
Takes a Unity Memory Profiler snapshot capturing native (C++) heap allocations and saves it as a timestamped .snap file in the profile/ folder
Sends a ping to all Nexus servers in the cluster simultaneously and prints individual round-trip times
Sends a ping to a specific Nexus server by name and prints the round-trip latency; used for testing inter-server connectivity
Checks whether the named or Steam-ID-specified player is currently online on any server in the Nexus cluster
Forces a refresh of the Nexus island layout, re-querying the zone controller for current island assignments
Initiates a Nexus transfer of the specified player to a named destination server or zone in the cluster
Reupload the map image to the nexus. Normally happens automatically at server boot. WARNING: This will lag the server!
Spawns and sends a patrol helicopter to the specified player or position, using the same logic as calltome but targeting another player
Orders the active patrol helicopter to fly to the calling admin position and attack there
Forces the active patrol helicopter to die immediately, triggering its death explosion and crash sequence
Spawns a patrol helicopter at the calling admin position, bypassing the normal random spawn logic; primarily used for testing
Orders the active patrol helicopter to immediately flee to a random distant location and disengage
Moves the active patrol helicopter to the calling admin current position without entering combat mode
Orders the active patrol helicopter to orbit around the calling admin current position
Orders the active patrol helicopter to orbit and strafe the calling admin position simultaneously
Orders the active patrol helicopter to resume normal patrol mode, following its randomised waypoint path across the map
Orders the active patrol helicopter to perform a strafing run on the calling admin current position
Triggers the helicopter puzzle sequence (approach, puzzle activation, reward) for testing the helicopter monument puzzle
Prints the combined world-space bounding box of the entity the calling admin is looking at; useful for verifying collider extents
Prints a sorted table of physics cells and how many colliders each contains; helps identify areas with excessive collider density causing physics slowdowns
Prints a sorted table of prefab names and their collider counts; identifies prefabs with unusually high collider counts for optimisation
(center vec3) (extents vec3)
Creates a human skull item named after the given player name (or a random name if none given) and gives it to the calling player's inventory
Creates a trophy head bag item for the specified entity type (by prefab name) and gives it to the calling player; used to generate mount-style trophy items for testing
Forces the specified player (by ID) to dismount from any vehicle or mountable they are currently seated in; admin/developer/cinematic mode only
Fills up liquid container items in your hotbar as well as any liquid containers you are looking at
Forces all players within a given radius (including the caller) to play one of the specified gesture names chosen at random; admin only; args: radius gesture1 [gesture2...]
Same as gesture_radius but excludes the calling admin from the gesture; forces all other players within the radius to perform a random gesture from the provided list
Forces the specified sleeping player (by ID) to enter the sleep state; admin/developer/cinematic mode only; also works on bots
Marks the calling player as hostile immediately, triggering the hostile timer as if they had attacked another player; useful for testing hostile-state dependent behaviour
Prints the calling player's current time-category presence flags: whether they are in wilderness, base, monument, swimming, boating, or flying
Prints detailed life-story stats for the calling player including time alive, distances travelled, damage taken/healed, kills, and per-weapon accuracy
optional param {player}
ragdolls
ragdolls everyone except player
Ragdolls a player you're looking at
Fully reloads all projectile weapons, flamethrowers, and liquid weapons in every belt slot of the calling player; useful for testing without consuming ammo
Resets the saved missions progress of the given player
Resets the saved missions progress of all player states on this server (online and offline players). Must be entered as "resetmissions_all Y" to execute.
Resets the PlayerState of the given player
Cancels any active gesture on all players within the specified radius of the calling admin; admin only; useful for stopping mass-gesture cinematics
Moves the specified player (by ID) to the given seat index on the vehicle they are mounted in; admin/developer/cinematic mode only
Admin-only: triggers the trap-think logic on the wildlife trap the calling player is looking at within 5 metres, simulating a catch attempt for testing
Empties the ammo from all projectile weapons, flamethrowers, and liquid weapons in every belt slot of the calling player; useful for testing reload behaviour
Wakes up the specified sleeping player (by ID), ending their sleep state immediately; admin/developer/cinematic mode only
Wakes up all sleeping players on the server at once; admin/developer/cinematic mode only; useful for clearing the sleeping player list after a wipe
Clears all cached entries from the asset pool matching the optional name filter
Clears all entries from the object memory pool matching the optional name filter; freed pooled objects are garbage collected
Clears all cached prefab instances from the prefab pool matching the optional filter, across client, server, and generic pools
Exports the current prefab pool contents to a prefabs.csv file listing pool ID, prefab short name, and instance count
Pre-warms the prefab pool by instantiating and pooling prefabs matching the optional filter up to the given count override
Prints a usage report for the BaseNetwork and ProtocolParser array pools, showing bucket sizes, capacities, and hit/miss stats
Prints a table of all asset pool entries showing asset type, current pooled count, and pool capacity
Prints a table of all object pool entries showing type, capacity, active count, peak usage, hit/miss counts, and spill counts; supports --json
Prints a table of all prefab pool entries showing prefab name, miss count, current count, target capacity, and push/pop counts; supports --json
Resets the peak-usage high-water-mark counter for all pools, allowing fresh measurement of maximum pool demand
Counts and prints all entities that use synchronous movement updates grouped by prefab, helping identify expensive per-frame entity movers
Flushes any buffered analytics events immediately to the analytics backend rather than waiting for the next scheduled flush
profile.perfsnapshot [delay=15, int] [name='Profile', str, no extension, max 32chars] [frames=10, int, max 10] [debug=false, dumps a binary snapshot as well]

profile.perfsnapshot_stream [name='Profile', str, no extension, max 32chars] [MainCap=32, int, max 256, buffer size for Main thread in Megabytes] [WorkerCap=8, int, max 256, buffer size for each Worker thread in Megabytes] [debug=false, dumps a binary snapshot as well]
Resets the profiler export interval timer, causing the next export to happen after a full ExportIntervalS from now
Starts recording a Unity Profiler binary log to a timestamped file in the profile/ folder; requires ENABLE_PROFILER build flag
Stops the active Unity Profiler binary log recording and finalises the file
Stops tracking of allocations
Params: [Name = 'Allocs'] [maxStackDepth = 16].
Clears the relay send queue (useful if things are going wrong with the relay server)
Attempts to restart the Rust Relay connection (may cause short lag)
Shutsdown the Rust Relay sever connection
Print off count of global building entities on the server
Print off count of trees to ensure server sent them all
Backup server folder
Returns the current number of bot (AI-controlled player) entities active on the server
Sends a video URL to all connected players, causing the in-game video player to open and play the specified video on every client
Kills all server-side bush entities within a given radius of the calling player (or a specified world position); args: radius [x y z]
Clears the loot spawn cache used to restrict loot into each era
Kills all server-side tree entities within a given radius of the calling player (or a specified world position); args: radius [x y z]
Clear the player reports list
Get the player combat log
Get the player combat log, only showing outgoing damage
Get info on player corpses on the server
Deletes items on the server that are not allowed in the era
Debug command: fills all chicken coops within 5 metres of the calling player to their maximum capacity
Prints the current server frame rate in frames per second
List the amount of reports players on the server have received
Prints all the Tool Cupboards on the server
Prints all the vending machines on the server
Returns the network protocol identifier string the server is currently using; clients must match this to connect
Prints a sorted table of network packet types and their cumulative call counts since logging was enabled; requires packetlog_enabled = true
Prints the number of player states currently held in the in-memory player state cache
Prints the total number of player state cache entries evicted since server startup; high values suggest the cache size should be increased
Prints the position of all players on the server
Print the current player eyes.
Print the current player position.
Print the current player center position.
Prints the current server incoming network read queue depth (packet count and total byte size)
Print the current player rotation.
Prints the current wipe id of the sav
Prints the current server outgoing network write queue depth (packet count and total byte size)
Reads and executes serverauto.cfg then server.cfg from the server's cfg folder, applying all saved convar values
Clear all static respawn points
Rescans the serveremoji folder, note that clients will need to reconnect to get the latest emoji
Prints a sorted table of RPC identifiers, their string names, and cumulative call counts; requires rpclog_enabled = true
Force save the current game
Send network update for all players
Show holstered items on player bodies
This sends a snapshot of all the entities in the client's pvs. This is mostly redundant, but we request this when the client starts recording a demo.. so they get all the information.
Starts a server
Stops a server
Unlock all static respawn points
Writes config files
Spawns a cargo ship and initiates the harbor docking test sequence at the specified docking path index; used to test cargo ship docking behaviour at harbors
Spawns a cargo ship and starts the cargo ship event immediately, bypassing the normal random event scheduler
Triggers a CH47 Chinook scientist event targeting the calling player position; optionally pass a start distance in metres (default 300)
Deletes all entities from every active population spawner on the server at once; effectively despawns all wildlife, NPCs, and resource nodes
Deletes all spawned entities belonging to the named population(s); pass one or more population names as arguments to target specific groups
Generates a debug spawn map for a named population, simulating up to the given number of spawn attempts and reporting how many would succeed; used to diagnose spawn point coverage
Immediately fills all spawn groups (monument NPC spawners, event spawners) to their maximum count without waiting for the normal tick interval
Immediately respawns all individually tracked entities that are currently missing, bypassing the normal tick_individuals delay
Immediately fills all population spawners to their target density; useful after a wipe or server restart to skip the gradual ramp-up period
Prints a spawn handler report listing all populations, their current count, target count, and fill percentage; pass true for detailed mode or a name filter as a second argument
Prints a table of current spawn scalar values including player fraction, excess, population rate, density, and group rate; pass --json for machine-readable output
Simulates a number of iterations on the closest loot container and sums up the items spawned
Server-side handler that spawns a dummy player entity at the given position and direction loaded with the Shields loadout, optionally holstering the shield; triggered by spawn.shielddummy
Forces an immediate recalculation of stability for all building blocks in the world; expensive on large bases
Calls in a supply drop to a specific grid coordinate or position; useful for testing supply crate loot tables and airdrop pathing
Spawns a supply drop at the calling admin or player position; the drop falls from the sky with a parachute like a naturally occurring airdrop
Sets the CPU core affinity mask for the process using comma-separated core indices or dash-separated ranges (e.g. 0,2-5)
Sets the OS process priority class (belownormal, normal, abovenormal, high); Idle and Realtime are blocked; not supported on OSX
Toggles auto-hover mode on the mini-helicopter the calling player is piloting, maintaining altitude automatically without pilot input
Print out boat drift status for all boats
Fixes up vehicles within 10m of the player by repairing to full hp, adding fuel to engines, and more
Destroys all boat vehicles (rowboats, RHIBs) currently spawned on the server
Destroys all modular car vehicles currently spawned on the server
Destroys all drone vehicles currently spawned on the server
Destroys all minicopter vehicles currently spawned on the server
Destroys all motorbike vehicles currently spawned on the server
Destroys all push bike vehicles currently spawned on the server
Destroys all scrap transport helicopter vehicles currently spawned on the server
Destroys all train vehicles currently spawned on the server
Immediately stops all train entities on the server, zeroing their speed; useful for clearing deadlocked train paths
Enabled/disables voice range boost for a player eg. ToggleVoiceRangeBoost sam 1
Lists all available volumetric cloud configuration asset names registered in the Climate instance
Loads and applies a named weather preset to the climate system; admin/developer only; server replicates the change to all clients
Loads a named volumetric cloud configuration and applies it to the climate override; admin/developer only; server replicates to clients
Prints a detailed report of the current weather state including fog, rain, wind, cloud, and all climate parameter values; admin/developer only
Resets all weather overrides and cloud configurations, restoring the dynamic weather system; admin/developer only; server replicates to clients
Clears the volumetric cloud configuration override and resets cloud settings to the dynamic weather system; server replicates to clients
Prints a list of all workshop-approved skins on the server with their item short names and approved skin IDs
Draws flat wireframe boxes in the world showing world bounds (red), terrain margin (yellow), deep sea bounds (cyan), and portal bounds (green/magenta) for the given duration in seconds
Prints a table of all monuments on the current map including type, display name, prefab path, and world position; admin/developer only
Renders a PNG of the current map's underwater labs, for a specific floor
Renders a high resolution PNG of the current map
Renders a PNG of the current map's tunnel network
Manually triggers a Christmas gift spawn pass, filling the world with gifts up to the giftsPerPlayer target for all connected players
Dumps a diagnostic snapshot into a numbered diagnostics/ subfolder including LOD group data and other engine diagnostics
Kills all currently running triggered event instances across all enabled event schedules; registered as eventschedule.killallevents
Triggers a named event schedule by partial name match; registered as eventschedule.triggerevent; lists available events if name is invalid or ambiguous
Ban an IP address from RCON, preventing it from connecting and kick any clients from this IP, this is permanent and persistent
Clear all failed login attempts
Clears all subscribers from the server.
Print a table of permanently banned IPs and networks. Use '--json' to return a JSON object
Print a table of connected RCON clients. Use '--json' to return a JSON object
Print a table of clients with failed passwords.  Use '--json' to return a JSON object
Un/Subscribes the current RCON connection to changes in server state. This could be a performance hit and should only be used if you need it.
Unban an IP address from connecting to RCON, will also remove all attempt history
Shows how many analytics events are pending upload
Dumps all available Unity Profiler recorder handles to CSV format showing name, category, unit type, and flags; useful for discovering available performance metrics
Simulates the provided number of hours on all farm animals within 10m
Server admin: forces all growable entities within 6m of the calling player to advance to their next growth stage instantly
Server admin: kills all growable entities within 6m of the calling player, removing them from the planter
Toggles the locked state of the handcuffs held by the calling admin player, switching between locked and unlocked
<gridSize> 5 <tileSpacing> 1.35
Prints the current state of all IO entity processing queues showing queue type and pending entity counts; admin-only
adds amount to convar
networkprofiler.serverprofile [time to profile(in seconds), min(0.1), max(1000), float]
Simulates the provided number of hours passing in the vending machine system
Print out all current price changes on the server
Resets the state of all discounts and surcharges from NPC vending machines
Kills all player boats that have more building blocks than the given threshold; used for server cleanup
Kills all player boats that have more deployed entities than the given threshold; used for server cleanup
Kills any IO entities deployed on boats. Not a fast command. Use sparingly when needed.
Kills any entities deployed on boats with non-convex colliders. Not a fast command. Use sparingly when needed.
Prints drag force debug data based on the angle between the boat heading and player look direction; admin-only
Prints a list of boats with non-convex collider deployables. Not a fast command. Use sparingly when needed.
Prints statistics about all player boats on the server including block counts, deployable counts, and resource totals
Opens all sails on the player boat directly in front of the calling admin player; admin-only dev command
Instantly kills the player boat directly in front of the calling admin player; admin-only dev command
Adds the calling player to their existing team, creating the team entry if needed
Creates a new team with the calling player and adds the specified player (by UID) to it; returns status string
Sends a fake team invite from the given team ID to the calling player; used for testing team invite UI flow
Adds a player to a team whether they are on the server or not
Toggles the sleep/wake state of the entity the calling admin player is looking at (within 5m); admin-only
Creates a new team with the calling player as leader; fails if teams are disabled (maxTeamSize == 0)
Wipes all relationship contacts data for every player on the server; admin-only
Wipes all relationship contacts data for the calling player
Ragdolls the ridable horse entity directly in front of the calling admin player; useful for testing horse physics and death states
Marks the horse directly in front of the calling admin player as for-sale, enabling the purchase interaction
Sets the breed index of the horse directly in front of the calling admin player to the given integer value
Sets the log verbosity level for a specific named RustLog channel; takes a channel name and optional level integer
Validates that all server occlusion network groups are correctly mapped to their occlusion data; reports any inconsistencies found
Tests occlusion visibility between two positions
Reassigns ownership of a sleeping bag (by entity ID) to the calling player; notifies both the old and new owner
Clears ownership of a sleeping bag (by entity ID), setting the owner ID to 0 and removing it from the old owner's bag list
Prints a table of all registered telephone entities showing their number, directory name, and world position
Spawns a Travelling Vendor at the calling player's position on the nearest ring road; registered as travellingvendor.spawn
Spawns a Travelling Vendor event instance; registered as travellingvendor.startevent
Removes the exclude layer configuration from all registered TriggerBase instances, resetting them to detect all layers
Applies the configured exclude layer mask to all registered TriggerBase instances to filter out unwanted layer detections
Toggles or explicitly sets VDDraw recording state; when enabled starts capturing DDraw commands for replay; when disabled stops recording
Wipe the backend customer stats data on all vending machines. Slow operation.
Wipe the backend stats data on all vending machines. Slow operation.
Prints a JSON list of all system, Windows, and IANA timezone identifiers available on the server machine
Prints the current wipe timer status including next wipe date, frequency, and time remaining
